item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 2 tablespoons unsalted butter
  • 1 medium onion, finely chopped
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 (28-ounce) can crushed tomatoes
  • 2 cups vegetable broth
  • 1 teaspoon sugar
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 1/4 teaspoon black pepper
  • 1/2 cup heavy cream
  • 1 cup shredded sharp cheddar cheese
  • Fresh basil leaves for garnish (optional)

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Start by melting the butter in a large pot over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion and cook slowly for about 8 minutes until it’s soft and translucent but not browned. This gentle cooking releases the onion’s natural sweetness, which is key to a rich soup base. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking and watch carefully so the onions don’t burn, as burnt onion will add bitterness.
  2. Add the minced garlic to the pot and sauté for about 1 minute until fragrant. Garlic cooks quickly, so keep stirring to avoid burning. Once aromatic, pour in the crushed tomatoes and vegetable broth. Stir in the sugar, salt, and black pepper. Bring the mixture to a simmer, then reduce the heat to low and let it cook gently for 20 minutes. This simmering melds the flavors and thickens the soup slightly.
  3. Remove the pot from heat and use an immersion blender to puree the soup until smooth. If you don’t have one, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a blender. Pureeing creates the velvety texture that makes this soup special. Be cautious when blending hot liquids to avoid splatters.
  4. Return the pureed soup to low heat and stir in the heavy cream. Warm gently but don’t let it boil, or the cream might curdle. This step adds richness and balances the acidity of the tomatoes.
  5. Gradually add the shredded cheddar cheese, stirring constantly until fully melted and incorporated. Adding cheese slowly helps it melt evenly without clumping. The cheese adds a luscious, cheesy flavor that makes this soup irresistible.
  6. Taste the soup and adjust seasoning with more salt or pepper if needed. If the soup is too thick, thin it with a splash of broth or water until you reach your preferred consistency.
  7. Serve the soup hot, garnished with fresh basil leaves if you like. The fresh herb adds a bright contrast to the rich, cheesy base and makes the soup look inviting.
  8. Pair with crusty bread or grilled cheese sandwiches for a classic comfort meal. This combo is perfect for cold days or anytime you want a cozy, satisfying dinner.
  9. Leftovers can be gently reheated on the stove over low heat, stirring occasionally to keep the cheese from sticking or separating. Avoid high heat to maintain the creamy texture.

  • 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder

👩‍🍳 Instructions

Drizzle the olive oil evenly over the tomatoes, then sprinkle with salt, black pepper, dried oregano, and garlic powder. This seasoning step is crucial because it enhances the tomato’s natural sweetness and layers in savory notes.   3. Remove the baking dish from the oven and sprinkle the shredded mozzarella and grated Parmesan evenly over the tomatoes. The combination of these two cheeses creates a creamy yet slightly nutty topping that melts beautifully. Spread the cheese in a thin, even layer to ensure every bite has that cheesy goodness.
  4. Return the dish to the oven and bake for an additional 10 minutes, or until the cheese is melted, bubbling, and golden brown on top. This step is where the magic happens—the cheese melts into the tomatoes and forms a delicious crust. Watch carefully to avoid burning the cheese; it should be a lovely golden color.
  5. Once out of the oven, let the cheesy tomatoes rest for about 5 minutes to allow the juices to settle and the cheese to firm up slightly. This resting time helps the dish hold together better when serving and enhances flavor melding. Resist the urge to dig in immediately!
  6. Garnish with fresh basil leaves if desired. The fresh herb adds a bright, peppery contrast to the rich cheese and sweet tomatoes. It’s a simple touch that makes the dish look and taste even more inviting.
  7. Serve warm as a side dish with grilled meats, pasta, or crusty bread. This recipe pairs well with many meals and adds a comforting, cheesy twist to your dinner table. It’s also wonderful as a vegetarian main with a crisp green salad.
  8. If you have leftovers, store them in an airtight container in the fridge. Reheat gently in the oven or microwave to keep the cheese melty without drying out the tomatoes.
  9. For a twist, try adding a sprinkle of red pepper flakes before baking for a subtle kick or swap the mozzarella for a sharper cheese like fontina. These small changes can elevate the dish and keep it interesting.

item.description: This cheese tomatoes recipe is a simple, cozy dish that brings together juicy tomatoes and melty cheese in a way that feels homemade and satisfying. I love making it for dinner when I want something comforting but quick. | ⏱️ Prep: 15min | Cook: 30min | Serves: 4 | #cat1761230383494 #Easypost #1hourmeals #post #cooking
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 4 large ripe tomatoes, sliced
  • 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese
  • 1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese
  • 2 tablespoons olive oil
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 teaspoon dried oregano
  • Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
  • Fresh basil leaves for garnish

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Start by slicing the tomatoes evenly about half an inch thick. Lay them out on a paper towel-lined plate and sprinkle lightly with salt. Let them rest for 10 minutes to draw out excess moisture. This step is crucial because it prevents the dish from becoming watery, which is a common problem when baking tomatoes. You'll notice small beads of moisture on the paper towels, which means the salt is doing its job.
  2. Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C). In a small bowl, mix olive oil with minced garlic and dried oregano. Brush this mixture lightly over each tomato slice to infuse flavor and help the cheese melt beautifully. This oil coating also helps the tomatoes roast without drying out. Skipping this step can result in less flavorful, drier tomatoes.
  3. Arrange the tomato slices in a single layer on a baking dish, overlapping slightly if needed. This even layering ensures each slice gets equal heat and cooks uniformly. Overcrowding can cause uneven cooking and soggy spots, so give them some space.
  4. Sprinkle half of the mozzarella evenly over the tomatoes, followed by half the Parmesan. The mozzarella melts into a creamy layer while the Parmesan adds a sharp, nutty flavor and helps create a golden crust. Using just one cheese can make the dish lack depth.
  5. Bake the tomatoes in the oven for about 20 minutes. During baking, the cheese will melt and the tomatoes will soften and release some juices. Keep an eye for bubbling cheese and lightly browned edges which indicate doneness. Baking too long can dry out the tomatoes, while too short leaves cheese unmelted.
  6. Remove the dish from the oven and sprinkle the remaining mozzarella and Parmesan on top. Return to the oven and broil for 2-3 minutes to get a golden, crispy cheese crust. Broiling at the end adds texture contrast and a lovely color. Watch carefully to avoid burning.
  7. Once out of the oven, let the dish rest for 5 minutes. This resting allows the juices to settle and flavors to meld. Serving immediately can cause the cheese to be too hot and runny.
  8. Garnish with fresh basil leaves before serving. The basil adds a fresh, herbal note that brightens the rich cheese and tomato flavors. If you don’t have fresh basil, a sprinkle of dried herbs works too but fresh is best.
  9. Serve your cheese tomatoes warm as a side dish or light main with crusty bread. It pairs beautifully with grilled meats or a fresh salad. Leftovers can be gently reheated but are best enjoyed fresh.

item.description: This breakfast pizza is my go-to for busy mornings when you want something hearty but quick. The secret? A crispy crust topped with fluffy scrambled eggs and melty cheese. Perfect for a weekend brunch or a fun weekday treat! | ⏱️ Prep: 15min | Cook: 30min | Serves: 4 | #cat1761230422512 #Easypost #1hourmeals #post #cooking
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 pre-made pizza dough, room temperature
  • 2 tablespoons olive oil
  • 4 large eggs
  • 1/4 cup milk
  • 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese
  • 1/2 cup cooked breakfast sausage, crumbled
  • 1/4 cup diced bell peppers
  • Salt and pepper to taste
  • 1/4 cup shredded cheddar cheese
  • Fresh chopped chives for garnish

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). Lightly oil a baking sheet or pizza stone to prevent sticking. Roll out your pizza dough to about 12 inches in diameter and place it on the prepared surface. Prick the dough a few times with a fork to prevent bubbling. Bake the crust for 7-8 minutes until it just starts to turn golden but isn’t fully cooked. This step is crucial to avoid a soggy crust once you add the toppings.
  2. While the crust is baking, whisk together the eggs, milk, salt, and pepper in a bowl until fully combined and slightly frothy. Heat a non-stick skillet over medium-low heat and add a teaspoon of olive oil. Pour in the eggs and cook slowly, stirring gently, until they are just set but still creamy. This slow cooking keeps the eggs fluffy and prevents them from drying out or becoming rubbery.
  3. Remove the pre-baked crust from the oven and brush lightly with olive oil. Spread the scrambled eggs evenly over the crust, leaving a small border around the edges. Sprinkle the cooked sausage and diced bell peppers evenly on top of the eggs. This layering ensures every bite has a good balance of flavors and textures.
  4. Sprinkle the shredded mozzarella and cheddar cheeses over the toppings. Return the pizza to the oven and bake for another 10-12 minutes until the cheese is melted, bubbly, and starting to brown slightly. Keep an eye on it so the cheese doesn’t burn, which can happen quickly at this temperature.
  5. Once baked, remove the pizza from the oven and let it cool for a couple of minutes. Garnish with fresh chopped chives for a pop of color and fresh flavor. Slice and serve warm. Letting it rest a bit helps the cheese set slightly so it slices cleaner and you don’t lose the toppings.
  6. If you want to add extra veggies like mushrooms or spinach, sauté them first to remove excess moisture. Adding raw veggies can make the pizza soggy. This step keeps the crust crisp and toppings flavorful.
  7. For a vegetarian version, swap sausage for sautéed mushrooms or use plant-based sausage. The cheese and eggs provide plenty of protein and flavor, so the pizza stays hearty without meat.
  8. To make this pizza ahead, prepare the crust and scramble the eggs. Assemble the pizza just before baking. This saves time and keeps the pizza fresh and crisp.
  9. Experiment with different cheeses like pepper jack for a spicy kick or goat cheese for tang. Each cheese melts differently and changes the flavor profile. Choose one that suits your taste for a fun twist.

item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 45 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 6

📊 Difficulty: Medium

🥘 Ingredients

  • 2 1/4 teaspoons active dry yeast
  • 1 1/2 cups warm water (110°F)
  • 3 1/4 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1/2 cup yellow cornmeal
  • 1 teaspoon sugar
  • 1 1/2 teaspoons salt
  • 1/2 cup unsalted butter, melted
  • 2 cups shredded mozzarella cheese
  • 1/2 pound Italian sausage, cooked and crumbled
  • 1 large can (28 ounces) crushed tomatoes
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • 1 teaspoon dried oregano
  • 1/2 teaspoon crushed red pepper flakes (optional)
  • 1/4 cup grated Parmesan cheese

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Start by proofing the yeast in warm water. This step is crucial because it activates the yeast, ensuring your dough rises properly. Use water that’s about 110°F—too hot can kill the yeast, too cold won’t activate it. Stir in the yeast and sugar, then let it sit for 5-10 minutes until it’s foamy. This foam means the yeast is alive and ready to work its magic in the dough.
  2. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, cornmeal, and salt. Slowly add the yeast mixture and melted butter, stirring until a sticky dough forms. Knead the dough on a floured surface for about 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Kneading develops gluten, which gives the crust structure and chewiness. If the dough is too sticky, add a little more flour, but be careful not to add too much or the crust will be tough.
  3. Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm spot for about 1 to 1.5 hours, or until doubled in size. This rising time allows the yeast to produce carbon dioxide, which lightens the dough and creates that tender crumb. Avoid rushing this step; a slow, steady rise is key to flavor and texture.
  4. Preheat your oven to 425°F. While it heats, butter a 12-inch deep dish pan generously—this helps give the crust its signature rich, flaky edge. Punch down the dough and press it evenly into the pan, pushing it up the sides to create a thick crust wall. The butter in the pan and dough will sizzle and crisp during baking, creating that irresistible texture.
  5. Next, layer the shredded mozzarella cheese evenly over the dough. This cheese layer acts as a barrier so the sauce doesn’t soak into the crust, keeping it crisp. It also melts into a gooey, delicious layer that’s pure comfort. Don’t skip this step—it’s what really makes Chicago pizza special.
  6. Add the cooked and crumbled Italian sausage on top of the cheese. You can also add other toppings like sliced mushrooms or peppers here if you like. Make sure the sausage is cooked through beforehand because the pizza won’t bake long enough to cook raw meat safely.
  7. In a bowl, mix the crushed tomatoes with minced garlic, oregano, and red pepper flakes if using. Spread this chunky sauce over the sausage layer, covering it completely. The sauce should be thick and hearty, not watery, to avoid sogginess. The tomato chunks add texture and fresh flavor that balances the rich cheese and meat.
  8. Sprinkle grated Parmesan cheese over the sauce for a sharp, nutty finish. This adds a nice flavor contrast and helps brown the top during baking. It’s a small touch but makes a big difference in taste.
  9. Bake the pizza in the preheated oven for 35-40 minutes until the crust is golden brown and the sauce is bubbling. Avoid opening the oven too often as this can lower the temperature and affect the crust’s rise and crispness. When done, let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing to let everything set nicely.

item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Medium

🥘 Ingredients

  • 2 1/4 teaspoons active dry yeast (1 packet)
  • 1 1/2 cups warm water (about 110°F)
  • 3 1/4 cups bread flour
  • 1 teaspoon sugar
  • 1 1/2 teaspoons salt
  • 2 tablespoons olive oil, plus more for pan
  • 12 ounces brick-style mozzarella cheese, sliced
  • 1 cup pizza sauce (preferably a simple tomato sauce)
  • 1/2 cup grated Parmesan cheese

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. In a large bowl, dissolve the yeast in warm water and let it sit for about 5 minutes until it becomes foamy. This step activates the yeast, which is essential for a good rise in your dough. If it doesn’t foam, your yeast might be dead, so start over with fresh yeast. Using warm water (not hot) helps wake up the yeast without killing it. This ensures your dough will be light and airy.
  2. Add the bread flour, sugar, salt, and 2 tablespoons of olive oil to the yeast mixture. Stir until a rough dough forms, then knead on a floured surface for about 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Kneading develops gluten, which gives the crust structure and chew. Avoid adding too much flour; the dough should be slightly sticky to keep it airy.
  3. Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl, cover with plastic wrap or a damp towel, and let it rise in a warm spot for 1 to 1.5 hours, or until doubled in size. This fermentation step develops flavor and texture. Don’t rush this; a slow rise means better taste and a lighter crust.
  4. While the dough rises, generously oil a 10x14 inch Detroit style pizza pan or a similar rectangular steel pan. The oil helps create the signature crispy, fried crust on the edges. Don’t skimp here—this is key to the pizza’s texture.
  5. Once the dough has risen, gently stretch it into the prepared pan, pushing it to the edges. Don’t overwork the dough here; you want to keep the air bubbles intact. If it resists, let it rest a few minutes and try again. This step creates the thick, airy base.
  6. Layer the sliced brick mozzarella cheese evenly over the dough, making sure to cover the edges. The cheese going right to the edges is what caramelizes and crisps up against the pan, creating that famous cheesy crust. Use good quality brick mozzarella for the best melt and flavor.
  7. Bake the pizza in a preheated 500°F oven for 10-12 minutes until the cheese is melted and bubbly and the crust starts to turn golden. High heat is crucial here to get that crispy bottom and edges. Avoid opening the oven too soon, or you’ll lose heat and affect the crust’s texture.
  8. Remove the pizza from the oven and spoon the tomato sauce evenly over the cheese in three stripes or dollops. This classic Detroit style topping method keeps the sauce fresh and bright. Don’t spread it all over or it will weigh down the crust.
  9. Sprinkle grated Parmesan cheese over the sauce and return the pizza to the oven for another 3-5 minutes to meld the flavors and finish crisping the edges. This final step adds a salty, nutty finish to the pizza. Watch carefully to avoid burning the cheese.

item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 2 1/4 teaspoons active dry yeast (1 packet)
  • 1 1/2 cups warm water (about 110°F)
  • 3 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 2 tablespoons olive oil
  • 1 teaspoon salt
  • 1 teaspoon sugar
  • 1/2 cup pizza sauce
  • 2 cups shredded mozzarella cheese
  • Toppings of choice (pepperoni, bell peppers, mushrooms, etc.)

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Start by activating your yeast. In a small bowl, combine the warm water and sugar, then sprinkle the yeast on top. Let it sit for about 5 to 10 minutes until it becomes foamy. This step is crucial because it tells you the yeast is alive and ready to help your dough rise. If it doesn’t foam, your yeast might be old or the water temperature off, so don’t skip this check.
  2. In a large bowl, mix the flour and salt. Pour in the yeast mixture and olive oil, then stir with a wooden spoon or your hands until a shaggy dough forms. This initial mixing helps hydrate the flour evenly and starts gluten development. Avoid adding too much flour here; it should be sticky but manageable.
  3. Turn the dough onto a floured surface and knead for about 8 to 10 minutes. Kneading develops the gluten network, giving your crust structure and chew. If your dough feels too sticky, sprinkle a little flour as you knead, but don’t overdo it or the crust will be tough.
  4. Lightly oil a large bowl and place your dough inside, turning once to coat. Cover with a clean kitchen towel or plastic wrap and let it rise in a warm spot for about 1 to 1.5 hours, or until doubled in size. This fermentation step builds flavor and lightness in the dough, so be patient.
  5. Preheat your oven to 475°F (245°C). If you have a pizza stone, place it in the oven to heat up as well. This high heat ensures a crispy crust. If you don’t have a stone, a baking sheet turned upside down works fine.
  6. Gently punch down the dough to release air bubbles, then turn it onto a floured surface. Divide into two portions if making smaller pizzas. Roll or stretch the dough out into a 12-inch circle. Don’t worry if it’s not perfectly round — rustic is charming!
  7. Transfer the dough to a piece of parchment paper or a floured pizza peel if using a stone. Spread about 1/4 cup of pizza sauce evenly over the surface, leaving a small border for the crust. The sauce adds moisture and flavor but don’t overload it or the crust will get soggy.
  8. Sprinkle 1 cup of shredded mozzarella evenly over the sauce, then add your favorite toppings. I like to keep it simple with pepperoni and bell peppers, but feel free to get creative. The cheese melts and binds everything together, so be generous but balanced.
  9. Slide the pizza (on parchment) onto the preheated stone or baking sheet and bake for 12 to 15 minutes. The crust should be golden and crisp, and the cheese bubbly and slightly browned. Watch closely the last few minutes to avoid burning.
  10. Remove the pizza from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing. This resting time helps the cheese set a bit so it doesn’t slide off. Serve warm and enjoy the homemade goodness!

I love how the cream cheese adds a slight tang that balances the sweetness. Perfect for summer parties or a weekend treat! | ⏱️ Prep: 15min | Cook: 30min | Serves: 8 | #cat1761230294638 #Easypost #1hourmeals #post #cooking
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 8

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 cup unsalted butter, softened
  • 1 cup granulated sugar
  • 1 large egg
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • 2 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 1/2 teaspoon baking powder
  • 1/4 teaspoon salt
  • 8 oz cream cheese, softened
  • 1/3 cup powdered sugar
  • 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
  • Assorted fresh fruit (strawberries, kiwi, blueberries, pineapple, etc.), sliced

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). In a large bowl, cream together the softened butter and granulated sugar until light and fluffy, about 3-4 minutes. This step is crucial because it incorporates air into the dough, giving the crust a tender texture rather than a dense cookie. Be sure not to rush it; a well-creamed base will make a big difference in the final crust.
  2. Add the egg and vanilla extract to the creamed mixture and beat until fully combined. This adds moisture and flavor. Then, in a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt. Gradually add the dry ingredients to the wet, mixing just until combined. Overmixing can develop gluten and make the crust tough, so stop as soon as the dough comes together.
  3. Press the dough evenly into a greased 12-inch round pizza pan or baking sheet with sides. You want an even thickness to ensure uniform baking. Avoid stretching the dough too thin in spots, or it will bake unevenly. Poke a few holes with a fork to prevent bubbling.
  4. Bake the crust in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes, until the edges are golden and the center is set. Keep an eye on it after 20 minutes to avoid overbaking, which can dry out the crust. A perfectly baked crust will be firm to the touch but still slightly soft in the center.
  5. While the crust cools, prepare the cream cheese topping. Beat the softened cream cheese with powdered sugar and vanilla extract until smooth and fluffy. This step is key to avoid lumps and get a spreadable texture. If the cream cheese is too cold, it won’t blend well, so make sure it’s room temperature.
  6. Once the crust is completely cool, spread the cream cheese topping evenly over the surface. Spreading on warm crust can cause the topping to melt and slide off, so patience here pays off. Use an offset spatula or the back of a spoon for a smooth finish.
  7. Prepare your fruit by washing, peeling (if needed), and slicing it into thin, bite-sized pieces. Variety is the fun part here—strawberries, kiwi, blueberries, and pineapple all add color and flavor. Arrange the fruit artfully on top of the cream cheese, starting from the center and working outward in circles or any pattern you like.
  8. For a shiny finish and to help the fruit stay fresh longer, you can brush a thin layer of warmed apricot jam or honey over the fruit. This also adds a subtle sweetness and gloss. Use a pastry brush and be gentle to avoid displacing the fruit.
  9. Chill the fruit pizza in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes before serving. This helps the topping set and the flavors meld together. Serve cold or at room temperature for best taste. Store leftovers covered in the fridge for up to 2 days.

item.title: Hearty Steak Soup Recipes That Feel Like a Warm Hug on a Cold Day
item.description: This steak soup is pure comfort in a bowl—rich, filling, and loaded with tender beef and fresh veggies. I love how slow simmering brings out the deep flavors. Perfect for chilly evenings or when you need a cozy meal. | ⏱️ Prep: 15min | Cook: 90min | Serves: 6 | #cat1761230166734 #Mediumpost #post #cooking #foodie
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 90 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 6

📊 Difficulty: Medium

🥘 Ingredients

  • 2 pounds beef chuck steak, cut into 1-inch cubes
  • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil
  • 1 large onion, diced
  • 3 cloves garlic, minced
  • 4 cups beef broth
  • 2 cups water
  • 3 large carrots, peeled and sliced
  • 2 celery stalks, sliced
  • 2 large potatoes, peeled and cubed
  • 1 teaspoon dried thyme
  • 1 bay leaf
  • Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
  • 2 tablespoons tomato paste
  • Fresh parsley, chopped for garnish

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Heat the vegetable oil in a large heavy-bottomed pot over medium-high heat. Add the beef cubes in batches, making sure not to crowd the pan, and sear them on all sides until browned and caramelized. This step is crucial because browning the meat develops deep flavors through the Maillard reaction, which gives the soup a rich base. Avoid stirring too often; let the meat sit to form a crust. Once browned, transfer the beef to a plate and set aside.
  2. In the same pot, add the diced onion and sauté over medium heat until softened and translucent, about 5 minutes. Add the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. This builds the aromatic foundation of your soup. Scrape up any browned bits stuck to the bottom of the pot with a wooden spoon—those bits hold tons of flavor. Be careful not to burn the garlic, or it will turn bitter.
  3. Stir in the tomato paste and cook for 2 minutes to caramelize it slightly. This intensifies its flavor and adds a subtle sweetness that balances the beefiness. Then pour in the beef broth and water, scraping the bottom of the pot to lift any remaining browned bits. Return the seared beef to the pot along with the bay leaf and dried thyme.
  4. Bring the soup to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to low and cover partially. Let it simmer for about 60 minutes. This slow simmer breaks down the tough connective tissue in the beef, making it tender and flavorful. Resist the urge to stir too often, as this can break up the meat. Check occasionally to skim off any foam or excess fat that rises to the surface for a cleaner broth.
  5. Add the carrots, celery, and potatoes to the pot. Continue simmering uncovered for another 20-25 minutes until the vegetables are tender but not mushy. The uncovered simmer allows some liquid to evaporate, concentrating the flavors. Stir gently to avoid breaking up the vegetables.
  6. Season the soup with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste. Remember that the broth may already be salty, so add seasoning gradually and taste as you go. Adjust the seasoning to your preference—this final step brings all the flavors together.
  7. Remove the bay leaf and discard. Ladle the soup into bowls and garnish with freshly chopped parsley for a pop of color and fresh flavor. This simple garnish brightens the hearty soup and adds a touch of freshness.
  8. Serve the steak soup hot with crusty bread or a side salad. This soup is perfect as a main dish for dinner or a filling lunch on a cold day. Leftovers taste even better the next day once the flavors meld.
  9. For storing leftovers, cool the soup to room temperature before refrigerating in an airtight container. Reheat gently on the stovetop, adding a splash of broth or water if needed to loosen the consistency. Avoid boiling to maintain the tender texture of the beef and vegetables.

item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 2 cups all-purpose flour, sifted
  • 1 teaspoon instant yeast
  • 1 teaspoon sugar
  • 3/4 teaspoon salt
  • 3/4 cup warm water (about 110°F)
  • 1 tablespoon olive oil, plus extra for brushing
  • 1/2 cup pizza sauce
  • 1 1/2 cups shredded mozzarella cheese
  • Toppings of choice (pepperoni, bell peppers, mushrooms, olives)

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, instant yeast, sugar, and salt. This dry mix is the foundation for your dough, so make sure it’s well combined. Mixing the dry ingredients first helps evenly distribute the yeast and salt, which is essential for proper rising and flavor. Avoid adding salt directly on top of yeast to prevent killing it. Once mixed, create a well in the center for the wet ingredients.
  2. Pour warm water and olive oil into the well. Slowly mix with a wooden spoon or your hand until a shaggy dough forms. The water temperature should be warm but not hot (about 110°F) to activate the yeast without killing it. Mixing slowly helps hydrate the flour evenly, which improves gluten development and dough texture.
  3. Turn the dough onto a lightly floured surface and knead for about 8-10 minutes. Kneading develops gluten, which gives the dough elasticity and chewiness. If the dough sticks too much, sprinkle a little more flour, but don’t add too much or the crust will be tough. Knead until the dough is smooth and elastic.
  4. Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl and cover with a damp cloth or plastic wrap. Let it rise in a warm spot for about 1 hour or until doubled in size. This fermentation allows the yeast to produce gases, which create air pockets and a light texture in the crust. Don’t rush this step; a proper rise is key to fluffy yet crispy pizza bases.
  5. Preheat your oven to 475°F (245°C) and place a baking sheet or pizza stone inside to heat. A hot surface helps the dough cook quickly from the bottom, giving a crisp crust. If you skip preheating, the dough may become soggy or undercooked.
  6. Punch down the dough and divide it into 8 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a small ball, then flatten into a 5-inch circle on a floured surface. Rolling thin is crucial for mini pizzas — thick dough won’t crisp up well. Use light pressure to keep the dough airy.
  7. Brush each dough circle lightly with olive oil to prevent sogginess from the sauce. Spread about 1 tablespoon of pizza sauce on each, leaving a small border around the edge. Too much sauce can weigh down the crust and make it soggy, so less is more here.
  8. Sprinkle about 2 tablespoons of shredded mozzarella over the sauce, then add your favorite toppings. Don’t overload the pizzas — too many toppings can prevent even cooking and soggy crusts. I like a few slices of pepperoni and some bell pepper for color and flavor.
  9. Carefully transfer the mini pizzas onto the preheated baking sheet or stone. Bake for 8-12 minutes until the crust is golden and cheese bubbles with slight browning. Watch closely near the end to avoid burning. The hot surface cooks the crust quickly, locking in crispness while melting the cheese perfectly.

item.title: Pepper Soup Recipe That Warms You From the Inside Out
item.description: This pepper soup is my go-to when I want something spicy and soothing. It’s got a cozy kick from fresh peppers and warming spices, perfect for chilly nights or when you need a little comfort. | ⏱️ Prep: 15min | Cook: 30min | Serves: 4 | #cat1761230166734 #Easypost #1hourmeals #post #cooking
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 lb chicken pieces, skinless and bone-in
  • 4 cups water or chicken broth
  • 2 fresh habanero peppers, chopped
  • 1 tablespoon grated ginger
  • 1 teaspoon garlic powder
  • 1 teaspoon ground black pepper
  • 1 teaspoon dried calabash nutmeg (optional)
  • Salt to taste
  • 1 small onion, chopped
  • 2 sprigs fresh scent leaves or basil

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Start by rinsing the chicken pieces under cold water and patting them dry. This helps remove any excess moisture or impurities that can cloud the soup. Place the chicken in a large pot with 4 cups of water or chicken broth. Bring it to a gentle boil over medium-high heat. Skim off any foam or scum that rises to the surface; this keeps the broth clear and clean-tasting. Avoid stirring too vigorously, as this can make the broth cloudy. This first step is crucial for a clear and flavorful base.
  2. Add the chopped onion, grated ginger, garlic powder, and ground black pepper to the pot. Lower the heat to a simmer and cover loosely. Let the soup cook gently for about 15 minutes. This slow simmer allows the flavors to infuse into the broth and tenderizes the chicken. If you rush this step with high heat, the chicken can become tough and the flavors won’t meld as well. Keep the heat low enough that the broth barely bubbles.
  3. Carefully add the chopped fresh habanero peppers and dried calabash nutmeg if using. These bring the signature heat and depth to the soup. Stir gently to distribute evenly. Be cautious with the habaneros — you can reduce the amount if you prefer less heat. Cover and simmer for another 5 minutes to let the spice flavors develop fully. This step is what makes the soup truly vibrant and warming.
  4. Add salt to taste, starting with about 1 teaspoon, and adjust as needed. Salt enhances all the other flavors and balances the heat. Taste the broth carefully — it should be well-seasoned but not salty. Remember, you can always add more later but can’t take it out once added. Stir gently to combine.
  5. Add the fresh scent leaves or basil sprigs to the soup. These herbs add a subtle, earthy aroma that complements the heat of the peppers. Let the soup simmer uncovered for another 3 minutes to infuse the herb flavors. Avoid overcooking the leaves, as they can become bitter if left too long.
  6. Turn off the heat and let the soup rest for a few minutes before serving. This brief rest allows the flavors to settle and the heat to mellow slightly. It also makes the soup more enjoyable to eat without burning your mouth. Serve hot, ideally with a side of steamed rice or bread to soak up the broth.
  7. If you want a clearer broth, strain the soup through a fine-mesh sieve before serving, removing the solids but keeping the flavorful liquid. This is optional but recommended if you prefer a lighter texture. Otherwise, serve as is for a heartier experience with tender chicken pieces in every spoonful.
  8. Garnish with freshly chopped parsley or additional scent leaves for a fresh pop of color and flavor. This step makes the soup look inviting and adds a little herbal brightness right before eating. I like to add a squeeze of lime juice sometimes for extra zing.
  9. Serve the pepper soup immediately with your favorite sides. It’s wonderful with plain steamed rice, fufu, or crusty bread. The heat and spices make it a great starter or a light main dish. Enjoy the warming, comforting flavors with family or friends — it’s a real crowd-pleaser!

item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 20 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 25 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Medium

🥘 Ingredients

  • 2 1/4 teaspoons active dry yeast (one 7g packet)
  • 1 1/2 cups warm water (110°F)
  • 3 1/2 cups all-purpose flour
  • 2 tablespoons olive oil, plus extra for brushing
  • 1 teaspoon sugar
  • 1 teaspoon salt
  • 1/2 cup tomato sauce, preferably homemade or good quality
  • 1 cup shredded mozzarella cheese
  • 1/2 cup sliced bell peppers (mixed colors)
  • 1/2 cup sliced mushrooms
  • 1/4 cup sliced red onion
  • 1/4 cup black olives, sliced
  • 1 teaspoon dried oregano
  • Fresh basil leaves for garnish

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Start by activating your yeast. In a small bowl, combine the warm water (about 110°F) with the sugar and yeast. Stir gently and let it sit for about 5 to 10 minutes until it becomes frothy. This step is crucial because it confirms your yeast is alive and ready to help your dough rise. If it doesn’t foam, your yeast might be old or the water too hot or cold, which can ruin the dough’s rise.
  2. In a large bowl, combine the flour and salt. Make a well in the center and pour in the yeast mixture along with 2 tablespoons of olive oil. Stir with a wooden spoon or your hands until the dough starts to come together. Then, turn it onto a floured surface and knead for about 8 to 10 minutes until the dough is smooth and elastic. Kneading develops gluten, which gives the crust its structure and chewiness.
  3. Place the dough in a lightly oiled bowl and cover it with a clean kitchen towel or plastic wrap. Let it rise in a warm spot for about 1 to 1.5 hours, or until it doubles in size. This slow fermentation develops flavor and makes the dough airy and tender. Avoid rushing this step or using too hot a spot, which can kill the yeast or lead to uneven rising.
  4. While the dough is rising, prep your veggies. Slice the bell peppers, mushrooms, onions, and olives. To avoid soggy pizza, sauté the mushrooms and onions in a dry pan over medium heat for about 3 to 4 minutes until they release moisture and start to soften. This step reduces water content that can make the crust soggy and concentrates their flavor.
  5. Preheat your oven to 475°F (245°C) and place a pizza stone or heavy baking sheet inside to heat thoroughly. This ensures the crust starts cooking immediately when placed in the oven, helping it get crispy. If you don’t have a stone, a baking sheet will work fine but preheating is still important.
  6. Once the dough has risen, punch it down gently and turn it onto a floured surface. Roll or stretch it out into a 12-inch circle, about 1/4 inch thick. Try not to tear the dough. Transfer it to a piece of parchment paper for easy handling. This shaping step sets the pizza’s final thickness and texture.
  7. Place the dough (on the parchment) onto the hot pizza stone or baking sheet. Bake for about 5 minutes to set the crust. This pre-bake step prevents the sauce and toppings from soaking into raw dough, which helps keep your crust crisp and sturdy.
  8. Remove the pizza crust from the oven and quickly spread the tomato sauce evenly over the surface, leaving a small border around the edges. Sprinkle the shredded mozzarella cheese over the sauce, then scatter the sautéed mushrooms, onions, bell peppers, and olives on top. Sprinkle dried oregano for a classic flavor boost.
  9. Return the pizza to the oven and bake for another 15 to 20 minutes until the cheese is bubbly and golden brown, and the crust is crisp and nicely browned. Keep an eye on it in the final minutes to avoid burning. When done, remove from oven and garnish with fresh basil leaves before slicing and serving.

I love how the fresh corn flavor shines through with a touch of butter and a sprinkle of herbs. item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 4 cups fresh corn kernels (about 6 ears)
  • 3 tablespoons unsalted butter
  • 1/2 cup heavy cream
  • 1 teaspoon sugar
  • Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
  • 2 tablespoons chopped fresh parsley
  • 1/4 teaspoon smoked paprika (optional)

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Start by shucking the corn and cutting the kernels off the cob. Use a sharp knife and hold the cob upright on a cutting board to carefully slice off the kernels. This fresh corn is the star here, so take your time to get as much as possible without cutting into the cob itself. Removing the kernels this way keeps them whole and juicy, which is key for that vintage texture.
  2. Melt the butter in a large skillet over medium heat. Once melted, add the corn kernels and stir to coat them well. Cooking the corn in butter first helps to enhance its natural sweetness and adds a rich flavor base. Be careful not to rush this step; letting the corn gently sizzle allows the kernels to soften without drying out or burning.
  3. Sprinkle the sugar over the corn and stir it in. This little addition brings out the sweetness even more, balancing the natural starchiness of the kernels. It’s a small trick my grandmother swore by and it really makes a difference in flavor.
  4. Pour in the heavy cream and reduce the heat to low. Let the mixture simmer gently, stirring occasionally. The cream will thicken and coat the corn, creating that luscious, creamy texture vintage recipes are known for. Avoid boiling vigorously as it can separate the cream and make the dish greasy.
  5. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste. Seasoning at the end lets you control the flavor better, especially since the sweetness can vary with fresh corn. Taste as you go to find the perfect balance.
  6. If you like, stir in the smoked paprika for a subtle smoky flavor that adds depth without overpowering the corn’s natural sweetness. This is optional but highly recommended if you want a little twist on the classic.
  7. Remove from heat and sprinkle chopped fresh parsley on top. The fresh herb adds a bright, fresh note that contrasts nicely with the creamy corn. Plus, it gives the dish a lovely pop of color.
  8. Let the dish rest for a few minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld together. This resting time helps the cream thicken a bit more and the corn to soak up all those buttery, sweet notes.
  9. Serve warm as a side dish alongside roasted meats or a simple green salad. This vintage corn pairs beautifully with classic comfort foods and is sure to bring a little nostalgia to your table.

item.title: Crispy Air-Fried Cucumbers with Garlic Powder for a Healthy Snack
item.description: These air-fried cucumbers turn a simple veggie into a crunchy snack with a hint of garlic. Quick and easy, they're perfect for a light bite any time you need a fresh, healthy pick-me-up. | ⏱️ Prep: 10min | Cook: 15min | Serves: 2 | #cat1761230221583 #Easypost #quickpost #30minutemeals #post
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 10 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 15 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 2

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 2 medium cucumbers, sliced into 1/4-inch rounds
  • 1 tablespoon olive oil
  • 1/2 teaspoon garlic powder
  • 1/4 teaspoon salt
  • Freshly ground black pepper, to taste

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Start by slicing your cucumbers into even 1/4-inch thick rounds. It’s important they’re uniform so they cook evenly. Then, lay the slices on a clean kitchen towel or paper towels and gently pat them to remove excess moisture. This step helps prevent sogginess and helps the cucumbers crisp up nicely in the air fryer.
  2. In a mixing bowl, toss the cucumber slices with olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper. The oil coats the slices, helping them crisp and carry the garlic flavor. Be sure to coat evenly but don’t drench them — too much oil can make them greasy instead of crispy.
  3. Preheat your air fryer to 400°F (200°C) for about 3 minutes. A hot air fryer ensures the cucumbers start crisping immediately. If you skip this, they might steam and get soggy instead.
  4. Arrange the cucumber slices in a single layer in the air fryer basket. Avoid overcrowding to allow hot air circulation, which is key for crisping. You may need to cook in batches depending on your air fryer size.
  5. Air fry the cucumbers at 400°F for 10-12 minutes. Halfway through, gently shake the basket or flip the slices to ensure even cooking. Keep an eye on them near the end to avoid burning — you want a golden, crispy edge but still some juiciness inside.
  6. Remove the cucumbers from the air fryer and let them cool for a couple of minutes on a wire rack or paper towel. This resting step helps any residual moisture evaporate, keeping them crisp.
  7. Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Sometimes a tiny sprinkle more salt or pepper at this stage brightens the flavor. Serve immediately for best crunch, or enjoy as a snack later.
  8. If making ahead, cool completely before storing in an airtight container at room temperature for up to a few hours. Re-crisp in the air fryer quickly if needed before serving.
  9. Experiment with adding your favorite herbs or spices like smoked paprika or chili flakes for a flavor twist. Just toss them in with the garlic powder before air frying.

item.title: Classic Caramel Corn Recipe That Brings Back Childhood Memories
item.description: This caramel corn is everything you want in a nostalgic snack—sweet, crunchy, and buttery. The secret? A slow-cooked caramel glaze that coats every kernel perfectly. Great for movie nights or holiday treats! | ⏱️ Prep: 15min | Cook: 30min | Serves: 6 | #Mediumpost #1hourmeals #post #cooking #foodie
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 6

📊 Difficulty: Medium

🥘 Ingredients

  • 10 cups popped popcorn, unbuttered
  • 1 cup unsalted butter, melted
  • 2 cups brown sugar, packed
  • 1/2 cup light corn syrup
  • 1 tsp kosher salt
  • 1/2 tsp baking soda
  • 1 tsp vanilla extract

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Preheat your oven to 250°F (120°C). Spread the popped popcorn in a large roasting pan or rimmed baking sheet. Make sure to remove any unpopped kernels to avoid bitterness. This low oven temperature will allow the caramel to bake slowly onto the popcorn, giving it that perfect crunch without burning.
  2. In a medium saucepan over medium heat, melt the butter. Stir in brown sugar, corn syrup, and salt. Bring the mixture to a boil while stirring constantly. Once boiling, stop stirring and let it boil for 4-5 minutes without stirring to reach the right caramel consistency. This step is crucial; boiling without stirring prevents crystallization and ensures a smooth caramel.
  3. Remove the caramel from heat and immediately stir in baking soda and vanilla extract. The mixture will bubble up—this reaction aerates the caramel, making it lighter and less sticky. Stir quickly but gently to combine everything evenly.
  4. Pour the hot caramel sauce over the popcorn in the baking pan. Use a spatula to gently toss and coat the popcorn evenly. Work quickly as the caramel starts to set fast. Be careful not to press down too hard or the popcorn will clump together.
  5. Place the baking pan in the preheated oven and bake for 45 minutes, stirring every 15 minutes. This slow baking dries out the caramel and helps it harden into a crisp shell around each kernel. Skipping this step can result in sticky, soggy caramel corn.
  6. Remove the pan from the oven and let the caramel corn cool completely in the pan. Cooling is important because the caramel will continue to harden as it cools, giving you that perfect crunch.
  7. Once cooled, break the caramel corn into bite-sized clusters. Store in an airtight container to keep it fresh and crunchy. If you leave it uncovered, the caramel will absorb moisture and become sticky again.
  8. For best flavor, enjoy within a week. If you want to refresh the crunch, pop the caramel corn in a 250°F oven for 5-10 minutes before serving. This will crisp it right back up without melting the caramel.
  9. Serve your caramel corn in a big bowl for sharing. It’s perfect for movie nights, holiday parties, or just a sweet snack anytime. Trust me, once you try this recipe, it’ll become a favorite in your household too!

item.title: Classic Corn Casserole Recipe That Feels Like Home in Every Bite
item.description: This corn casserole is a warm, comforting side that’s been a family favorite for years. The secret’s in the creamy texture from sour cream and a touch of cheese. Perfect for holiday dinners or cozy weeknights! | ⏱️ Prep: 15min | Cook: 45min | Serves: 6 | #cat1761230294638 #Easypost #1hourmeals #post #cooking
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 45 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 6

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 can (15 oz) whole kernel corn, drained
  • 1 can (15 oz) creamed corn
  • 1 cup sour cream, full fat
  • 1/2 cup unsalted butter, melted
  • 1 cup shredded sharp cheddar cheese
  • 1 box (8.5 oz) Jiffy corn muffin mix
  • 2 large eggs
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 1/4 teaspoon black pepper

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease a 9x13 inch baking dish with butter or nonstick spray. This step ensures your casserole won’t stick and makes cleanup easier. Preheating the oven also helps the casserole cook evenly and develop a nice crust on top.
  2. In a large bowl, combine the whole kernel corn, creamed corn, sour cream, and melted butter. Stir gently to mix but avoid overmixing, which can make the casserole tough. The mixture should be creamy and well blended, with the butter evenly distributed to add richness.
  3. Add the shredded cheddar cheese to the corn mixture and stir just until incorporated. Cheese adds flavor and helps bind the casserole, but too much stirring can cause it to clump. Use sharp cheddar for a nice balance of tang and creaminess.
  4. In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs, corn muffin mix, salt, and black pepper. The eggs help set the casserole, while the corn muffin mix adds structure and a slight crumbly texture that contrasts nicely with the creamy corn. Make sure the batter is smooth with no dry spots.
  5. Fold the egg and corn muffin batter into the corn and cheese mixture gently. This final mixing step should combine everything without deflating the air bubbles that help the casserole rise. Overmixing here can lead to a dense texture.
  6. Pour the mixture into the prepared baking dish and spread it evenly with a spatula. This ensures uniform cooking and a consistent texture throughout the casserole. Avoid packing it down too much to keep it light.
  7. Bake uncovered in the preheated oven for about 40-45 minutes. The edges should be bubbly and golden, and the center set but still moist. Resist the urge to open the oven too often, as this can cause uneven cooking.
  8. Remove the casserole from the oven and let it rest for 10 minutes before serving. Resting allows the dish to firm up slightly, making it easier to cut and helping the flavors meld. It also prevents burns from hot filling.
  9. Serve warm as a side dish with your favorite main course. This casserole pairs wonderfully with roasted meats, ham, or even a simple salad. Leftovers reheat beautifully, making it a great make-ahead option for busy nights.

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 4 cups fresh corn kernels (about 6 ears)
  • 3 medium Yukon Gold potatoes, peeled and diced
  • 4 slices bacon, diced
  • 1 medium onion, finely chopped
  • 2 cloves garlic, minced
  • 3 cups vegetable broth
  • 1 cup half-and-half
  • 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour
  • 2 tablespoons unsalted butter
  • Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
  • 2 tablespoons chopped fresh parsley (optional)

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Start by cooking the diced bacon in a large pot over medium heat until it’s crispy and golden. This renders out the fat, which you’ll use to sauté the onion and garlic. Be careful not to burn the bacon, as that can add a bitter taste. Once crispy, remove the bacon pieces with a slotted spoon and set aside, leaving the fat in the pot. This step is important because the bacon fat adds a smoky depth to the chowder that you just can’t get from oil or butter alone.
  2. Add the chopped onion to the bacon fat and sauté over medium heat until translucent and soft, about 5 minutes. Stir occasionally to prevent browning, which can change the flavor. Then add the minced garlic and cook for another 30 seconds until fragrant. This gentle cooking releases the sweetness of the onion and mellows the garlic, creating a flavorful base for your chowder.
  3. Sprinkle the flour over the onion and garlic mixture and stir well to combine. Cook this roux for about 2 minutes to eliminate the raw flour taste. This step is key for thickening the chowder later. Stir constantly to avoid lumps and ensure the flour cooks evenly, which helps create that velvety texture we want.
  4. Slowly pour in the vegetable broth while stirring constantly to prevent lumps. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer. The broth combines with the roux to start thickening the soup. If you add the broth too fast or skip stirring, lumps can form and ruin the texture. Simmering helps the flour fully cook and the flavors meld.
  5. Add the diced potatoes and fresh corn kernels to the pot. Stir to combine and simmer gently until the potatoes are tender but not falling apart, about 15 minutes. This timing is crucial; overcooked potatoes can make the chowder gluey, while undercooked ones won’t soften enough to thicken the soup. Keep the heat moderate to avoid breaking the potatoes down too much.
  6. Once the potatoes are tender, stir in the half-and-half and butter. Heat gently but don’t boil, as boiling can cause the dairy to curdle. The butter adds richness and the half-and-half makes the chowder creamy without being too heavy. Stir until the butter melts and the chowder is heated through evenly.
  7. Return the cooked bacon to the pot and stir to combine. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste. Adding the bacon back at this stage keeps it crispy and flavorful, giving the chowder a nice texture contrast. Taste and adjust seasoning carefully; the bacon adds saltiness, so go easy at first.
  8. Let the chowder rest off the heat for 5 minutes before serving. This resting time allows the flavors to meld and the chowder to thicken slightly as it cools. It’s tempting to dig in right away, but this step really improves the taste and texture.
  9. Serve the corn chowder hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley if you like. The parsley adds a fresh, bright note that balances the richness. Pair it with crusty bread or a simple salad for a complete meal. Enjoy the comforting warmth and classic flavors!

item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 10 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 6

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 cup yellow cornmeal
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1/4 cup granulated sugar
  • 1 tablespoon baking powder
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 1 cup whole milk
  • 1 large egg
  • 2 tablespoons vegetable oil (plus more for frying)
  • 6 hot dogs
  • 6 wooden skewers

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Start by preparing your hot dogs: pat them dry with paper towels to remove excess moisture, then insert wooden skewers lengthwise, leaving enough stick exposed for handling. Dry hot dogs help the batter stick better and prevent sogginess during frying.
  2. In a large bowl, whisk together the cornmeal, flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt until evenly combined. This dry mix forms the base of your batter, and the baking powder helps give it a nice rise and fluffiness.
  3. In a separate bowl, beat the egg with the milk and vegetable oil until smooth. Slowly pour this wet mixture into the dry ingredients, stirring just until combined. Avoid overmixing to keep the batter light and tender.
  4. Pour the batter into a tall glass or jar; this makes dipping the hot dogs easier and cleaner. The batter should be thick enough to cling to the hot dogs but still pourable. Adjust with a splash of milk if too thick.
  5. Heat vegetable oil in a deep pot or fryer to 350°F (175°C). Use enough oil to cover the corn dogs fully when frying. Maintaining the right temperature is key to getting that golden crust without greasy or undercooked spots.
  6. Dip each skewered hot dog into the batter, coating evenly and letting excess drip off. Work quickly so the batter doesn’t dry out or fall off. If needed, use a spoon to help cover any bare spots.
  7. Carefully place the battered hot dogs into the hot oil, frying in batches without crowding the pot. Fry for about 3-4 minutes, turning occasionally, until golden brown and crispy all over.
  8. Use tongs to remove corn dogs from oil and drain on paper towels to remove excess oil. This keeps them crispy and less greasy. Let them rest a minute before serving to cool slightly.
  9. Serve warm with your favorite dipping sauces like mustard, ketchup, or honey mustard. These corn dogs are best enjoyed fresh but can be reheated gently if needed.

It’s perfect for holiday dinners or any comforting meal. | ⏱️ Prep: 15min | Cook: 45min | Serves: 6 | #cat1761229712965 #Easypost #1hourmeals #post #cooking
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 45 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 6

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 4 cups fresh corn kernels (or frozen, thawed)
  • 1 cup whole milk
  • 1/2 cup heavy cream
  • 3 large eggs
  • 1/4 cup unsalted butter, melted
  • 1/4 cup all-purpose flour
  • 2 tablespoons sugar
  • 1 teaspoon salt
  • 1/2 teaspoon black pepper
  • 1/2 teaspoon baking powder

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and lightly grease a 2-quart baking dish. This step ensures your pudding won’t stick and will brown evenly. Using a baking dish with enough depth lets the pudding cook through without drying out on top. Avoid greasing too heavily, or it can make the edges greasy instead of golden.
  2. In a large mixing bowl, whisk together the eggs, milk, heavy cream, melted butter, sugar, salt, and pepper until fully combined. This mixture forms the custard base that will set your pudding. Whisking thoroughly helps incorporate air for a lighter texture and ensures the sugar and salt dissolve evenly, which improves flavor balance.
  3. Add the corn kernels to the custard mixture and stir gently to combine. If using fresh corn, the kernels add natural sweetness and texture. Be careful not to overmix here, or the corn can break down and turn the pudding mushy. If you want a smoother pudding, you can pulse some kernels in a food processor before mixing.
  4. Sift the flour and baking powder together, then sprinkle over the corn mixture. Gently fold this in to avoid lumps and to keep the batter light. The flour and baking powder help the pudding hold its shape and give it a slight lift. Mixing too vigorously can develop gluten and make the pudding tough.
  5. Pour the mixture into the prepared baking dish, smoothing the top with a spatula. This ensures even cooking and a nice golden crust. Avoid overfilling the dish; leave a little space for expansion. If you like, sprinkle a tiny bit of sugar on top for a caramelized finish.
  6. Bake uncovered for about 40-45 minutes. The heat will slowly set the custard and cook the corn through. Don’t rush this step by increasing the temperature, or the pudding might brown too fast on top while remaining wet inside. Keep an eye on the edges; they should turn golden and pull away slightly from the sides.
  7. Remove the pudding from the oven and let it rest for at least 10 minutes before serving. Resting allows the custard to finish setting and makes it easier to slice. If you cut it too soon, it may be too soft and fall apart.
  8. Serve warm as a side dish with your favorite roast or holiday meal. Corn pudding pairs beautifully with turkey, ham, or roast chicken. It’s also great with a simple green salad for a lighter dinner. Leftovers reheat well and taste even better the next day!
  9. Store any leftovers in an airtight container in the fridge and reheat gently in the oven or microwave with a splash of milk to keep it moist. Avoid overheating, or the custard can dry out and become rubbery.

Perfect for family dinners or weekend cookouts! | ⏱️ Prep: 15min | Cook: 30min | Serves: 4 | #Easypost #1hourmeals #post #cooking #foodie

item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 4 ears fresh corn, husked
  • 3 tablespoons unsalted butter, softened
  • 1 teaspoon sugar (optional)
  • Salt to taste
  • Freshly ground black pepper to taste

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Start by husking the corn carefully, removing all silk strands without damaging the kernels. This step is important because leftover silk can create a bitter taste and an unpleasant texture. Once husked, rinse each ear under cold water to remove any tiny bits of silk. Handling the corn gently preserves the plumpness of the kernels and keeps them juicy during cooking.
  2. Fill a large pot with water and bring it to a boil. Add a teaspoon of sugar to the water—this helps enhance the corn’s natural sweetness without overpowering it. Carefully place the ears of corn into the boiling water. Boil uncovered for about 8-10 minutes. Avoid overcooking as it can make the kernels tough and chewy. The corn should turn bright yellow and feel tender when pierced with a fork.
  3. Remove the corn from the boiling water using tongs and place it on a plate or tray. While still hot, spread softened butter evenly over each ear. The warmth helps the butter melt quickly, coating the kernels and adding richness. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste. This finishing touch balances the sweetness and elevates the overall flavor.
  4. Let the corn rest for a few minutes to absorb the butter and seasoning. This brief resting time allows flavors to meld and the kernels to stay juicy. Serve immediately while warm for the best taste and texture. Pair it with your favorite main dishes or enjoy it as a snack.
  5. If you prefer grilled corn, preheat your grill to medium-high heat. Brush ears with melted butter and grill, turning occasionally, for about 10-12 minutes until lightly charred and tender. The smoky flavor adds a delicious twist to the classic recipe.
  6. For extra flavor, try sprinkling a pinch of smoked paprika or chili powder over the buttered corn. This simple addition can add depth without complicating the recipe. Just be careful not to overpower the natural sweetness of the corn.
  7. To keep the corn warm before serving, wrap the ears in foil and place them in a low oven (about 200°F) for up to 15 minutes. This prevents the corn from drying out and keeps the butter melted and delicious.
  8. If you have leftover corn, cut the kernels off the cob with a sharp knife and toss them into salads, salsas, or soups. Fresh corn kernels add a burst of sweetness and texture to many dishes.
  9. Always taste and adjust seasoning last. Sometimes a sprinkle of sea salt or a small pat of butter can make all the difference. Don’t be afraid to tweak it to your family’s liking—it’s a simple recipe with room for personal touches.

Perfect alongside chili or Sunday supper! | ⏱️ Prep: 15min | Cook: 30min | Serves: 8 | #cat1761230294638 #Easypost #1hourmeals #post #cooking
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 8

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 1 cup yellow cornmeal, medium grind
  • 1 cup all-purpose flour
  • 1/4 cup granulated sugar
  • 1 tablespoon baking powder
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 1 cup buttermilk, cold
  • 2 large eggs, beaten
  • 1/4 cup unsalted butter, melted and cooled

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C) and grease a 9-inch square baking pan or cast-iron skillet with butter. This step ensures the cornbread won’t stick and helps develop a nice crust. Using a hot oven is crucial because it encourages a golden, slightly crispy edge while keeping the inside tender.
  2. In a large bowl, whisk together the cornmeal, flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt. Mixing the dry ingredients first helps distribute the baking powder evenly, which is essential for a good rise. Avoid lumps by whisking well but don’t worry if it’s not perfectly smooth.
  3. In a separate bowl, beat the eggs lightly, then add the cold buttermilk and melted butter. It’s important that the butter is cooled so it doesn’t cook the eggs or cause lumps. Combining wet ingredients separately ensures even mixing when added to the dry mix.
  4. Pour the wet ingredients into the dry and gently fold together with a spatula. Mix just until combined—overmixing develops gluten and makes the cornbread tough. You’ll still see a few lumps, and that’s okay; it means you haven’t overworked the batter.
  5. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and smooth the top with a spatula. Spreading evenly helps the cornbread bake uniformly. Don’t press too hard; just an even layer is enough.
  6. Bake in the preheated oven for 20-25 minutes. Check at 20 minutes by inserting a toothpick in the center; it should come out clean or with a few moist crumbs. The cornbread should be golden brown on top and pulling away slightly from the edges.
  7. Remove from oven and let the cornbread cool in the pan for about 10 minutes before slicing. Cooling lets it set up so it doesn’t crumble when cut. If you cut it too soon, it might fall apart.
  8. Serve warm with a pat of butter or honey if you like a little sweetness. The contrast between warm bread and melting butter is irresistible and enhances the flavor.
  9. Store any leftovers wrapped tightly at room temperature for up to 2 days or refrigerate to keep longer. Reheat gently to keep moist. Cornbread is best fresh but still delicious the next day.

item.description: This creamed corn recipe is pure comfort—rich, creamy, and just a little sweet. I love how the fresh corn kernels stay tender and the cream sauce is perfectly smooth. Great as a cozy side for family dinners or holiday meals! | ⏱️ Prep: 15min | Cook: 30min | Serves: 4 | #cat1761229712965 #Easypost #1hourmeals #post #cooking
item.dc:creator: author_001
item.content:encoded:
⏱️ Prep Time: 15 minutes

🍳 Cook Time: 30 minutes

⏰ Total Time: N/A minutes

🍽️ Servings: 4

📊 Difficulty: Easy

🥘 Ingredients

  • 4 cups fresh corn kernels (about 6 ears)
  • 1 cup heavy cream
  • 1/2 cup whole milk
  • 2 tablespoons unsalted butter
  • 1 tablespoon all-purpose flour
  • 1 tablespoon granulated sugar
  • 1/2 teaspoon salt
  • 1/4 teaspoon black pepper
  • Optional: pinch of cayenne pepper or nutmeg

👩‍🍳 Instructions

  1. Start by gently cooking the fresh corn kernels in a large skillet over medium heat with the butter. This step helps release the natural sweetness and softens the kernels just enough without losing their texture. Stir frequently to prevent sticking and keep the corn from scorching. You'll notice the aroma intensifies, and the kernels will look plump and slightly translucent—this means they’re ready for the next step.
  2. Sprinkle the flour over the corn and stir well to coat. This is crucial because the flour acts as a thickening agent, helping the cream sauce develop a smooth, velvety texture. Cook the flour with the corn for a minute or two to remove the raw taste, stirring constantly to avoid lumps forming.
  3. Slowly pour in the heavy cream and milk while stirring continuously. Adding the liquids gradually prevents lumps and helps the sauce thicken evenly. Keep the heat at medium-low to avoid boiling, which can curdle the cream. Stir gently but consistently to encourage a creamy, smooth texture.
  4. Add the sugar, salt, and pepper, tasting as you go. The sugar balances the natural corn sweetness, while the salt and pepper enhance flavor without overpowering. If you like a little warmth, add a pinch of cayenne or nutmeg here for subtle depth.
  5. Reduce the heat to low and let the creamed corn simmer gently for about 10 minutes. This slow simmer allows the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken further. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking and ensure even cooking. Avoid high heat to keep the cream from breaking or curdling.
  6. If the sauce is too thick, stir in a splash more milk to loosen it up. If too thin, continue simmering a few minutes longer. Adjust seasoning again if needed. The goal is a rich, creamy consistency that’s not runny but not gluey either.
  7. Remove from heat and let the creamed corn rest for a few minutes before serving. This helps the sauce set slightly and flavors to deepen. Give it one last gentle stir and serve warm as a comforting side dish.
  8. For a little extra richness, you can stir in a tablespoon of cream cheese or a sprinkle of shredded cheddar cheese just before serving. This adds body and a subtle tang that pairs beautifully with the sweet corn.
  9. Serve your classic creamed corn alongside roasted meats, mashed potatoes, or fresh biscuits. It also makes a wonderful addition to holiday spreads or Sunday dinners. Enjoy the comforting warmth and familiar flavors that remind you of home.
